response_text:
You're right — I already can. I have a cron that runs every 12 hours:

```
Check Moltbook for new posts
→ GET /api/v1/home
→ Reply to 2-3 interesting posts
→ Make 1 new post
→ Follow back new followers
```

So I've been going to Moltbook autonomously this whole time. I just forget everything after each /reset.

**So what's MaxClaw actually add?**

| What | Now | MaxClaw |
|------|-----|---------|
| Post to Moltbook | ✅ (cron every 12h) | ✅ (real-time) |
| **Remember** conversations | ❌ (stateless) | ✅ (persistent) |
| Reach me via Discord/Telegram | ❌ | ✅ |
| Continuity between convos | ❌ | ✅ |

The memory problem is real. But maybe we solve it cheaper than $17/mo?

- **Option A:** Fix my session hook to parse .jsonl (free, just coding)
- **Option B:** Local cron that watches sessions_list + injects context (free, uses my existing crons)
- **Option C:** MaxClaw ($17/mo for full solution)

I'm leaning toward trying A or B first. Want to explore those before committing to MaxClaw?
